content_en:
Abu Bakr: the Best of the Prophet's Companions:

Ahlus-Sunnah (the Sunnis) have unanimously agreed that the best people after Prophet Muhammad, peace and blessings be upon him, are Abu Bakr, then ~ Umar, then * Uthmaan, then “Ali, then the rest of the ten Companions whom the Prophet gave the glad tidings of entering Paradise, then the Companions of the Prophet who participated in the battle of Badr, then those who participated in the battle of Uhud, then those who swore the pledge of allegiance to the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, and the rest of the Companions. The fact that Abu Bakr As-Sideeq is the best of the Prophet's Companions is confirmed by many Hadiths. Here are some of them:

[bn *Umar may Allah be pleased with him, is quoted to have said: "We used to compare Muslims in forms of who was better during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him. We used to regard Abu Bakr as the best, then “Umar, and then *Uthmaan." (Reported by AlAl-Bukhari.)

In the version of At-Tabraani: "And when the Prophet,

peace and blessings be upon him, was informed about this, he found no. fault with it." Abu Hurayrah, may Allah be pleased with him, narrates: "We, the Companions of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, used to say while we were sitting together. The best of men in the nation of Islam after the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, are Abu Bakr, then ~Umar, then ~Uthmaan, then we used to keep silent."(Reported by Ibn* Asaakir.)

Jabir, may Allah be pleased with him, reports that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "Never has the sun risen nor set on a person better than Abu Bakr." (Reported by At-Tabaraani and others.)

“Amr Ibn AlAas, may Allah be pleased with him, is reported to have said: "I asked the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, 'Who is the dearest person to you amongst all of mankind?’ He replied, "*Aa'ishah.’ [then asked, ‘Who amongst men?' He answered, ‘Her father (i.e. Abu Bakr).' I asked further, ‘And who is next?’ He said, “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab.' The Narrator said: The Prophet

then enumerated some other men. (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Moslem.)

Abu Bakr: the Bravest Companion:

*Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, is reported to have once asked the people, "Tell me who is the bravest man?" "You" replied the people. He then asked them, 'Never did I fight anyone except that I put him to rout; but tell me who is the bravest man.' The people said, 'We do not know, tell us who is he?' "Abu Bakr" replied Ali "in the battle of Badr, we built a shelter for the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, and then asked who is willing to stay with the Prophet so that none of the polytheists may dare to attack the Prophet. Non of us had the courage to accept this, save for Abu Bakr who came forward sword in hand, unsheathing it over the Prophet's head; and none of the polytheists was able harm the Prophet. So he is the bravest man. * Ali added: "I saw the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, surrounded by Quraysh, threatened by some and gripped violently by the garment by others; they said: "Are you the one who calls for the unity of gods?!" “Ali said: "By Allah, none could intervene to save the Prophet but Abu Bakr: he fought, threatened and pushed them aside, while saying, 'Woe unto you! Would you kill a man because he says, My Lord is Allah?" Then he (Abu Bakr) raised his mantle over the Prophet, and wept until his beard was wet. Then ~ Ali said, ‘Tell me by Allah, who is better: the believer of Pharaoh's people or Abu Bakr?' The people kept silent and ~ Ali said, “Why do you not answer me? By Allah, a single hour spent by Abu Bakr better than a thousand hours spent by the believer of Pharaoh's people: that was aman who kept his faith secret, while this is a man who made his faith public." (Reported by Al-Bazzaar.)

Narrating another story of Abu Bakr's bravery, ~ Urwah Ibn Az-Zubayr, may Allah be pleased with him, says: | asked ~Abdullaah Ibn ~ Amr Ibn Al-* Aas, "Tell me about the worst thing the disbelievers did to the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him." He said, "While the Prophet was praying in the Hijr (roofless ceiling) of the Ka* bah, ~Uqbah Ibn Abi Mu‘ ayt put his garment around the Prophet's neck and throttled him violently. Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, came and caught him by his shoulder and pushed him away from the Prophet and said, "Would you kill a man for saying, ‘Allah is my Lord’?” (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

~Aa'ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, is reported to have said: When the number of Muslims reached thirty nine, Abu Bakr asked the permission of Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, to preach Islam publicly. On his persistent request the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, gave his consent, and all of them went to the mosque (the Ka*bah) to preach. People dispersed in the corners of the mosque, each preaching his folk. Then Abu Bakr gave a Khutbah (sermon); thus he was the first ever Khateeb (orator) to call to Allah and His Messenger. When the disbelievers heard him, they fell upon the Muslims from all sides. Abu Bakr was kicked, thrashed with shoes, trampled under feet and handled most roughly and savagely. (Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.)

~Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, is reported to have said: When Abu Bakr embraced Islam, he showed his faith openly, and called others to Allah and His Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him. (Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.)

Abu Bakr: the Most Generous Companion:

Allah the Almighty says:

"But those most devoted to Allah shall be removed fur from it (i.e. the Hell-Fire). Those who spent! Their wealth for increase in self purification. "(Al-Layl: 17-18) Ibn Al-Jawzi states: This verse was revealed in reference to Abu Bakr.

Abu Hurayrah may Allah be pleased with him reports that the Prophet, peace and blessing be upon him said: "I did not benefit from wealth as | benefit from the wealth of Abu Bakr." Whereupon Abu Bakr burst into tears and said, "My person and wealth are entirely at your service, O Messenger of Allah!" (Reported by Ahmad.)

*Aa'ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, is reported to have said: “Abu Bakr freed seven people all of whom were tortured in the Cause of Allah. "(Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.)

“Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, may Allah be pleased with him, narrates: The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, asked us to give out charity in Allah's Cause, and I had some wealth. Then 1 said, "It's time for me to outdo Abu Bakr today." So I presented half of my total wealth, The Prophet asked me, "Have you left anything for your family?" "An equal amount of it," [ replied. Then came Abu Bakr with all that he had. The Prophet asked him, "O Abu Bakr! Have you left anything for your family?" "Allah and His Messenger" was the reply. [ said, "Never will I outdo Abu Bakr." (Reported by Al-Tirmidhi.)

Abu Hurayrah, may Allah be pleased with him, reports the Prophet, peace and blessing be upon him, to have said:

"Never did anyone do us a favor but was rewarded, except Abu Bakr: he did us a favor which will be rewarded only by Allah on the Day of Judgment. And I did not benefit from any wealth as I benefit from the wealth of Abu Bakr.” (Reported by Al-Tirmidhi.)

Abu Bakr: the Most Knowledgeable and Intelligent Companion:

Al-Nawawi states in his Tahdheeb: Scholars have deduced that Abu Bakr is the most knowledge of the Prophet's Companions on the basis of the authentic Hadith reported by Al-Bukhari and Moslem: "When Allah's Messenger passed away and Abu Bakr became the caliph, some Arab renegade (reverted to disbelief). Abu Bakr, decided to declare war against them. *Umar, said to him, "How can you declare war against these people when Allah's Messenger says: "/ have been ordered (by Allah) to fight against people till they bear witness that there is no god but Allah, and if they do so their lives and property shall be protected, unless (they do acts that are punishable) is accordance with Islam, and their reckoning will be with Allah Almighty." Abu Bakr said, "By Allah! [ will fight those who distinguish between Salaah (prayer) and Zakaah (obligatory charity), because Zakaah is the compulsory right to be taken from one's property (according to Allah's Commands). By Allah, if they refuse to give me even the cord used for hobbling the feet of a camel, which they used to pay as Zakaah at the time of Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, I would fight them for withholding it." Then ~Umar said, "By Allah it was nothing, but Allah guided Abu Bakr's heart towards the decision.”

Al-Nawawi adds: Sheikh Abu Ishaq and others quote this Hadith in support of their view that Abu Bakr was the last

knowledgeable of the Prophet's Companions. They argue that all the companions feel short of judging the situation until Abu Bakr stated his opinion which was correct and they acted in accordance with it.

Ibn “Umar was asked, "Who amongst the Companions used to give Fatwa during the lifetime of the Prophet, peace and_ blessings be upon him?" He said, "Abu Bakr and * Umar, and no one else".

On the authority of Abu Sa*eed Al-Khudryy, may Allah be pleased with him: The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, addressed the people saying: "There is a slave whom Allah, Exalted and Glorified be He, offered the choice between the good afterworld and that which is with Him, and the slave has chosen that which is with Allah." Abu Bakr wept, and we were astonished at his weeping caused by what the Prophet mentioned. We learned later on that Allah's Messenger himself was the person who was given the choice, and that Abu Bakr knew best of all of us. Allah's Messenger added: "The most bountiful of men unto me_ in his companionship and his sacrifice of his wealth is Abu Bakr. If I were to choose a bosom friend, I would choose Abu Bakr - but companionship and brotherhood in faith bind us until Allah unites us in His presence. All the gates of the mosque should be closed except the gate of Abu Bakr.”
content_id:
Abu Bakr: Sahabat Terbaik Nabi:

Ahlus-Sunnah telah sepakat bahwa manusia terbaik setelah Nabi Muhammad, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, adalah Abu Bakr, kemudian 'Umar, kemudian 'Utsman, kemudian 'Ali, kemudian sepuluh sahabat lainnya yang diberi kabar gembira oleh Nabi akan masuk surga, kemudian para sahabat Nabi yang ikut serta dalam perang Badr, kemudian mereka yang ikut serta dalam perang Uhud, kemudian mereka yang berbaiat kepada Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dan sahabat-sahabat lainnya. Kenyataan bahwa Abu Bakr Ash-Shiddiq adalah sahabat terbaik Nabi dikuatkan oleh banyak hadith. Berikut sebagian di antaranya:

Ibnu 'Umar radhiyallahu 'anhu diriwayatkan berkata: "Kami biasa membandingkan kaum muslimin tentang siapa yang lebih baik pada masa hidup Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. Kami menganggap Abu Bakr sebagai yang terbaik, kemudian 'Umar, dan kemudian 'Utsman." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

Dalam riwayat Ath-Thabrani: "Dan ketika Nabi,

shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, diberitahu tentang hal ini, beliau tidak menemukan kesalahan padanya." Abu Hurayrah, radhiyallahu 'anhu, meriwayatkan: "Kami, para sahabat Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, biasa berkata ketika kami duduk bersama. Manusia terbaik dalam umat Islam setelah Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, adalah Abu Bakr, kemudian 'Umar, kemudian 'Utsman, kemudian kami diam." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu 'Asakir.)

Jabir, radhiyallahu 'anhu, meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Tidak pernah matahari terbit maupun terbenam atas seseorang yang lebih baik dari Abu Bakr." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ath-Thabrani dan lainnya.)

'Amr bin Al-'Ash, radhiyallahu 'anhu, diriwayatkan berkata: "Aku bertanya kepada Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, 'Siapakah orang yang paling engkau cintai di antara seluruh manusia?' Beliau menjawab, ''Aisyah.' Aku bertanya, 'Siapa di antara kaum laki-laki?' Beliau menjawab, 'Ayahnya (yakni Abu Bakr).' Aku bertanya lagi, 'Dan siapa selanjutnya?' Beliau bersabda, ''Umar bin Al-Khaththab.'" Perawi berkata: Nabi

kemudian menyebutkan beberapa laki-laki lainnya. (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Abu Bakr: Sahabat Paling Berani:

'Ali, radhiyallahu 'anhu, diriwayatkan pernah bertanya kepada orang-orang, "Beritahu aku siapa laki-laki paling berani?" "Engkau," jawab mereka. Beliau kemudian bertanya, 'Aku tidak pernah melawan siapa pun kecuali aku mengalahkannya; tetapi beritahu aku siapa laki-laki paling berani.' Mereka berkata, 'Kami tidak tahu, beritahu kami siapa dia?' "Abu Bakr," jawab 'Ali, "dalam perang Badr, kami membangun tempat perlindungan untuk Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, lalu bertanya siapa yang bersedia tinggal bersama Nabi agar tidak ada seorang pun kaum musyrikin yang berani menyerang Nabi. Tidak seorang pun di antara kami yang berani menerima hal ini, kecuali Abu Bakr yang maju ke depan dengan pedang di tangan, menghunus pedangnya di atas kepala Nabi; dan tidak seorang pun kaum musyrikin mampu menyakiti Nabi. Maka dialah laki-laki paling berani." 'Ali menambahkan: "Aku melihat Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dikepung oleh Quraisy, diancam oleh sebagian dan dicengkeram dengan kasar pada pakaiannya oleh yang lain; mereka berkata: 'Apakah kamu yang menyeru penyatuan tuhan-tuhan?!'" 'Ali berkata: "Demi Allah, tidak ada yang bisa turun tangan menyelamatkan Nabi kecuali Abu Bakr: beliau bertempur, mengancam dan mendorong mereka ke samping, sambil berkata, 'Celaka kalian! Apakah kalian hendak membunuh seseorang karena dia berkata, Tuhanku adalah Allah?'" Kemudian beliau (Abu Bakr) mengangkat jubahnya menutupi Nabi, dan menangis hingga jenggotnya basah. Kemudian 'Ali berkata, 'Beritahu aku demi Allah, siapakah yang lebih baik: orang beriman dari kaum Fir'aun atau Abu Bakr?' Orang-orang terdiam dan 'Ali berkata, "Mengapa kalian tidak menjawabku? Demi Allah, satu jam yang dihabiskan oleh Abu Bakr lebih baik dari seribu jam yang dihabiskan oleh orang beriman dari kaum Fir'aun: orang itu adalah seseorang yang menyembunyikan imannya, sementara ini adalah seseorang yang menampakkan imannya secara terang-terangan." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bazzar.)

Meriwayatkan kisah lain tentang keberanian Abu Bakr, 'Urwah bin Az-Zubair, radhiyallahu 'anhu, berkata: Aku bertanya kepada 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'Ash, "Beritahu aku tentang hal terburuk yang dilakukan kaum kafir terhadap Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam." Beliau berkata, "Ketika Nabi sedang salah di Hijr (bagian Ka'bah yang tidak beratap), 'Uqbah bin Abi Mu'aith meletakkan pakaiannya di leher Nabi dan mencekiknya dengan keras. Abu Bakr, radhiyallahu 'anhu, datang dan menangkap bahunya lalu mendorongnya menjauh dari Nabi dan berkata, "Apakah kamu hendak membunuh seseorang karena dia berkata, 'Allah adalah Tuhanku'?" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

'Aisyah, radhiyallahu 'anha, diriwayatkan berkata: Ketika jumlah kaum muslimin mencapai tiga puluh sembilan, Abu Bakr meminta izin Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, untuk menyebarkan Islam secara terbuka. Atas permintaannya yang terus-menerus, Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, memberikan persetujuannya, dan mereka semua pergi ke masjid (Ka'bah) untuk berdakwah. Orang-orang berpencar di sudut-sudut masjid, masing-masing berdakwah kepada kaumnya. Kemudian Abu Bakr memberikan khutbah; maka beliau adalah Khathib (orator) pertama yang menyeru kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya. Ketika kaum kafir mendengarnya, mereka menyerang kaum muslimin dari segala penjuru. Abu Bakr ditendang, dipukul dengan sepatu, diinjak-injak dan diperlakukan dengan sangat kasar dan kejam. (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu 'Asakir.)

'Ali, radhiyallahu 'anhu, diriwayatkan berkata: Ketika Abu Bakr memeluk Islam, beliau menampakkan imannya secara terbuka, dan mengajak orang lain kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu 'Asakir.)

Abu Bakr: Sahabat Paling Dermawan:

Allah Ta'ala berfirman:

"Tetapi orang yang paling bertakwa akan dijauhkan darinya (yakni neraka). Yaitu yang menafkahkan hartanya (di jalan Allah) untuk membersihkan dirinya." (Al-Lail: 17-18) Ibnu Al-Jawzi menyatakan: Ayat ini diturunkan berkenaan dengan Abu Bakr.

Abu Hurayrah radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Aku tidak pernah mengambil manfaat dari harta siapa pun sebagaimana aku mengambil manfaat dari harta Abu Bakr." Maka Abu Bakr menangis tersedu-sedu dan berkata, "Diriku dan hartaku sepenuhnya untuk engkau, wahai Rasulullah!" (Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad.)

'Aisyah, radhiyallahu 'anha, diriwayatkan berkata: "Abu Bakr membebaskan tujuh orang yang semuanya disiksa di jalan Allah." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu 'Asakir.)

'Umar bin Al-Khaththab, radhiyallahu 'anhu, meriwayatkan: Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, memerintahkan kami untuk bersedekah di jalan Allah, dan aku memiliki beberapa harta. Maka aku berkata, "Inilah saatnya aku melampaui Abu Bakr hari ini." Maka aku menyerahkan setengah dari seluruh hartaku. Nabi bertanya kepadaku, "Apakah engkau menyisakan sesuatu untuk keluargamu?" "Sejumlah yang sama," jawabku. Kemudian datanglah Abu Bakr dengan semua yang dimilikinya. Nabi bertanya kepadanya, "Wahai Abu Bakr! Apakah engkau menyisakan sesuatu untuk keluargamu?" "Allah dan Rasul-Nya," jawabnya. Aku berkata, "Aku tidak akan pernah bisa melampaui Abu Bakr." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi.)

Abu Hurayrah, radhiyallahu 'anhu, meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda:

"Tidak pernah seseorang berbuat kebaikan kepada kami kecuali kami membalasnya, kecuali Abu Bakr: beliau berbuat kebaikan kepada kami yang hanya akan dibalas oleh Allah pada Hari Kiamat. Dan aku tidak pernah mengambil manfaat dari harta siapa pun sebagaimana aku mengambil manfaat dari harta Abu Bakr." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi.)

Abu Bakr: Sahabat Paling Berilmu dan Cerdas:

An-Nawawi menyatakan dalam kitab Tahdzib-nya: Para ulama menyimpulkan bahwa Abu Bakr adalah sahabat Nabi yang paling berilmu berdasarkan hadith shahih yang diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim: "Ketika Rasulullah wafat dan Abu Bakr menjadi khalifah, sebagian orang Arab murtad (kembali kepada kekufuran). Abu Bakr memutuskan untuk menyatakan perang terhadap mereka. 'Umar berkata kepadanya, 'Bagaimana engkau bisa menyatakan perang terhadap orang-orang ini padahal Rasulullah bersabda: "Aku diperintahkan (oleh Allah) untuk memerangi manusia hingga mereka bersaksi bahwa tiada tuhan selain Allah, dan jika mereka melakukannya maka jiwa dan harta mereka akan terlindungi, kecuali (jika mereka melakukan perbuatan yang patut dihukum) sesuai dengan Islam, dan perhitungan mereka ada pada Allah Yang Maha Kuasa."' Abu Bakr berkata, 'Demi Allah! Aku akan memerangi mereka yang membedakan antara salah dan zakah, karena zakah adalah hak wajib yang harus diambil dari harta seseorang (sesuai perintah Allah). Demi Allah, jika mereka menolak memberiku bahkan seutas tali yang digunakan untuk mengikat kaki unta, yang biasa mereka bayarkan sebagai zakah pada masa Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, niscaya aku akan memerangi mereka karena menahannya.' Kemudian 'Umar berkata, 'Demi Allah, tidak lain Allah telah membimbing hati Abu Bakr menuju keputusan itu.'"

Al-Nawawi menambahkan: Sheikh Abu Ishaq dan ulama lainnya mengutip hadith ini sebagai dalil pendapat mereka bahwa Abu Bakr adalah yang paling berilmu di antara para sahabat Nabi. Mereka berargumen bahwa seluruh sahabat tidak mampu menilai situasi tersebut hingga Abu Bakr menyatakan pendapatnya yang ternyata benar, dan mereka pun bertindak sesuai dengannya.

Ibn Umar pernah ditanya, "Siapakah di antara para sahabat yang biasa memberikan fatwa pada masa hidup Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam?" Beliau menjawab, "Abu Bakr dan Umar, dan tidak ada yang lain."

Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri, radhiyallahu 'anhu: Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, berkhutbah kepada orang-orang dengan bersabda: "Sesungguhnya ada seorang hamba yang Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala beri pilihan antara kebaikan dunia dan apa yang ada di sisi-Nya, dan hamba itu telah memilih apa yang ada di sisi Allah." Abu Bakr pun menangis, dan kami heran dengan tangisannya yang disebabkan oleh apa yang Nabi sebutkan. Kemudian kami mengetahui bahwa Rasulullah sendiri adalah orang yang diberi pilihan tersebut, dan bahwa Abu Bakr adalah yang paling mengetahui di antara kami semua. Rasulullah menambahkan: "Orang yang paling dermawan kepadaku dalam persahabatan dan pengorbanan hartanya adalah Abu Bakr. Seandainya aku boleh memilih seorang khalil (kekasih dekat), niscaya aku akan memilih Abu Bakr — tetapi persahabatan dan persaudaraan dalam iman mengikat kita hingga Allah menyatukan kita di hadirat-Nya. Hendaklah semua pintu masjid ditutup kecuali pintu Abu Bakr."
